body.thing: ground-loop master-off suppresses restarts when halted
body.intention: run_once() consults HaltRegistry('ground_loop'); halted -> no scan/tick/supervise; not halted -> full cycle

red_run.summary:
halt = _FakeHalt(True, "updating ground_loop")
        gl = GroundLoop(halt_registry=halt)
        calls: list[str] = []
        _instrument(gl, calls)
    
        gl.run_once()
    
>       assert calls == [], (
            f"master-off must suppress all restart work when halted; ran: {calls}"
        )
E       AssertionError: master-off must suppress all restart work when halted; ran: ['scan', 'tick', 'supervise']
E       assert ['scan', 'tick', 'supervise'] == []
E         
E         Left contains 3 more items, first extra item: 'scan'
E         Use -v to get more diff

tests/test_ground_loop_master_off.py:41: AssertionError
=========================== short test summary info ============================
FAILED tests/test_ground_loop_master_off.py::test_master_off_suppresses_all_restart_work_when_halted
1 failed in 0.11s

body.narrative: Master-off Akien requested: flat-file HaltRegistry gate so the lowest-level loop stops restarting peers during an update.
body.why: A hollow run_once ignoring the flag keeps restarting peers while halted; the calls==[] assertion fails (authentic red). Green proves the gate actually suppresses restarts.
